text:
I know tomorrow feels big, but you're going to get through this. You've already shown courage just by facing it head-on, and that matters more than you might realize right now.

The medical team doing your procedure has done this many times before. They know what they're doing, and they're going to take good care of you. Try to be gentle with yourself today—rest if you can, do something that brings you a little comfort, and remember that it's okay to feel whatever you're feeling.

When you wake up tomorrow, this will be behind you. And on the other side of it is recovery and healing. You're stronger than you think, and you don't have to feel ready to actually be ready.

I'm thinking of you. You've got this.
